key_topics: Operations and Algebraic Thinking: addition and subtraction within 100
Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies
Even and odd numbers within 20
Arrays: using repeated addition to find the total
Number and Operations in Base Ten: understanding place value to 1000
Reading and writing numbers to 1000 using base-ten numerals, number names, expanded form
Comparing three-digit numbers using <, >, =
Fluently add and subtract within 100
Adding up to four two-digit numbers
Adding and subtracting within 1000 using concrete models
Mentally add or subtract 10 or 100 from any number 100-900
Measurement: measuring and estimating lengths in standard units (inches, feet, cm, m)
Relating addition and subtraction to length
Telling and writing time to the nearest five minutes
Money: solving word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, pennies
Data: picture graphs, bar graphs, line plots
Geometry: recognizing and drawing shapes, partitioning rectangles into rows/columns
competences: Fluently add and subtract within 20 from memory
Understand place value for three-digit numbers
Measure and estimate lengths using standard units
Tell time to the nearest 5 minutes on analog and digital clocks
Solve word problems involving money (coins and bills)
typical_mistakes: Not regrouping when adding two-digit numbers (e.g., writing 17 as two separate digits)
Subtracting the smaller digit from the larger regardless of position
Confusing centimeters and inches when measuring
Errors in reading clock (confusing hour and minute hands)
Not counting change correctly (starting from wrong coin value)

key_topics: Reading Literature: theme and central message, character response to events
Describing how characters respond to challenges
Comparing and contrasting two or more versions of the same story
Reading Informational Text: main purpose, key details
Describing the connection between steps in a technical procedure
Phonics: decoding two-syllable words, long-vowel spellings, r-controlled vowels
Common prefixes and suffixes (un-, re-, -ful, -less)
Reading grade-level text fluently and accurately
Writing: opinion writing with reasons, informative writing, narrative writing with details
Using linking words (because, and, also, but)
Research: participating in shared research projects
Language: collective nouns, irregular plurals, reflexive pronouns, adjectives and adverbs
competences: Read and comprehend grade 2 literature and informational texts
Write coherent opinion, informative, and narrative paragraphs
Use phonics knowledge to decode unfamiliar multisyllabic words
Demonstrate command of grammar conventions in writing
typical_mistakes: Difficulty with irregular plural nouns (feet, children, mice)

key_topics: Properties of matter: solid, liquid, gas — how matter changes
Heating and cooling: changes in matter
Plants and their needs: water, sunlight, soil, air
Life cycles of plants and animals
Habitats and ecosystems: what living things need to survive
Earth's surface: landforms, bodies of water
Maps: using simple maps to locate landforms
competences: Describe and classify matter by observable properties
Explain how heating and cooling change matter
Describe plant and animal life cycles
Identify how animals use their habitat to meet needs
typical_mistakes: Confusing physical change with chemical change
Thinking all life cycles are identical

key_topics: Communities: urban, suburban, rural differences
Local government: leaders, laws, citizen responsibilities
Economics: producers and consumers, trade
History: significant American historical figures
Geography: cardinal directions, simple maps and globes
Cultural diversity in America
Contributions of various ethnic groups to America
competences: Compare urban, suburban, and rural communities
Identify roles of local government leaders
Explain basic economic concepts (goods, services, trade)
Use cardinal directions and simple maps
typical_mistakes: Difficulty distinguishing urban vs. suburban vs. rural
